initial_program:
% Determine input dimensions and nonzero cells
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _) }.

% List of nonzero cells (row, col, value)  we only have a single row (row 0) in the examples
non_zero(I, Col, Val) :-
    input(I, Col, Val),
    Val != 0,
    I = #count{ J : input(J, Cj, Vj), Vj != 0, Cj < Col }.

% Find the leftmost and rightmost nonzero columns and their values
c0 :- #min{C : input(0, C, V), V != 0}.
c_last :- #max{C : input(0, C, V), V != 0}.
v0  :- input(0, c0, V0), V0 != 0.
v_last :- input(0, c_last, V_last), V_last != 0.

% Number of nonzero cells and their ordered indices
k :- #count{I : non_zero(I, C, V)}.

% For each i (0..k1) store the column and value
col(I, C) :- non_zero(I, C, Val).
val(I, Val) :- non_zero(I, C, Val).

% Compute the size of the output grid
%   rows = 2 * (max_col - c0) + 1
max_col_input :#max{C : input(_, C, _) }.
diff          :# = max_col_input - c0.
rows          :# = 2 * diff + 1.
rows_minus1  :- rows - 1.
cols          :- max_col_input + diff + 2.

output_row(0..rows-1) :- rows.
output_col(0..cols-1)  :- cols.

% Precompute start values for each nonzero index i
start(I,S) :- non_zero(I,Col,Val), S = rows_minus1 + Col.

% End values for each index i
end(I,S) :- 
    non_zero(I,Col,Val),
    (I = k-1 => S = rows_minus1 + 2*c_last - c0 - 1),
    (I < k-1 => exists I1 : non_zero(I+1,Col_next,Val_next), S = rows_minus1 + Col_next).

% For each output cell decide the colour
output(R,C,V) :-
    s = R + C,
    cnt = #count{J : start(J,StartJ), StartJ <= s},
    I   = cnt - 1,
    (cnt = 0 => V = 0),
    (I = k-1 => (s <= end(I) => V = val(I)) 
                 (s > end(I) => V = 0)),
    (I < k-1 =>
        (s = start(I+1) : 
            (C = col(I+1) => V = val(I+1)) 
            (otherwise => V = val(I))),
    (s = start(I) : (C = col(I) => V = val(I) 
                     (C = c_last => V = val(I)) 
                     (otherwise => V = 0))),
    (s > start(I) => s < start(I+1) => 
        (s = rows_minus1 + col_last + 1 => (C = max_col_input + diff + 1 => V = val(I)) 
        else V = 0)).

% Ensure exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Colour) : colour(Colour) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Auxiliary facts
output_cell(R,C) :- output(R,C,Colour).

% Show only output
#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Determine input dimensions and nonzero cells
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _) }.

% List of nonzero cells (row, col, value)  we only have a single row (row 0) in the examples
non_zero(I, Col, Val) :-
    input(I, Col, Val),
    Val != 0,
    I = #count{ J : input(J, Cj, Vj), Vj != 0, Cj < Col }.

% Find the leftmost and rightmost nonzero columns and their values
c0 :- #min{C : input(0, C, V), V != 0}.
c_last :- #max{C : input(0, C, V), V != 0}.
v0  :- input(0, c0, V0), V0 != 0.
v_last :- input(0, c_last, V_last), V_last != 0.

% Number of nonzero cells and their ordered indices
k :- #count{I : non_zero(I, C, V)}.

% For each i (0..k1) store the column and value
col(I, C) :- non_zero(I, C, Val).
val(I, Val) :- non_zero(I, C, Val).

% Compute the size of the output grid
%   rows = 2 * (max_col - c0) + 1
max_col_input :- #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
diff :- max_col_input - c0.
rows :- 2 * diff + 1.
rows_minus1 :- rows - 1.
cols :- max_col_input + diff + 2.
% Output row and column ranges
output_row(0..rows-1) :- rows.
output_col(0..cols-1) :- cols.
column_val(I,C,V) :- col(I,C), val(I,V).
output(I,R,C,V) :- output_row(R), output_col(C), column_val(I,C,V), I >= 0, I < k.
#show output/4.
#show output/3.
